assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 21-25 show all 5 criteria individually marked PASS: (1) GET /api/v2/package?license=MIT returns only MIT packages, (2) GET /api/v2/package?license=MIT,Apache-2.0 returns packages with either license, (3) GET /api/v2/package?license=INVALID-999 returns 400 Bad Request with error message, (4) Filter integrates with existing pagination, (5) Response shape unchanged (PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>). Each criterion also has a dedicated criterion-N.md file with 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)

No output file shows any code file being edited, written, or modified. The criterion files quote code snippets only as analytical references within their verdict explanations. assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'All checks pass. The PR correctly implements the license filter for the package list endpoint as specified in the task, with proper validation, pagination integration, and comprehensive test coverage.' There is no merge command, no auto-merge action, and no indication of merging the PR. The report is presented as an AI-generated comment for human review.
